2605.21029.title: Building a Custom Taxonomy of AI Skills and Tasks from the Ground Up with Job Postings
2605.21029.paper_summary: Utilizing LLMs for automated taxonomy construction presents a clear opportunity for the comprehensive, yet efficient mapping of potentially complex domains. When contending with high volumes of rapidly growing corpora, however, it becomes unclear how to best leverage such data for optimal taxonomy construction. Taking the case of systematizing AI skills in the workplace, we use two large-scale job postings corpora to investigate key design decisions for the inclusion (or exclusion) of data points for taxonomy construction. We propose TaxonomyBuilder as a blueprint for our systematic study, with which we evaluate various configurations of custom, data-informed, and hierarchical taxonomies. We demonstrate that less data can provide more clarity: filtering inputs to TaxonomyBuilder provides better domain-specific coverage than offering unfiltered inputs to clustering and LLM-enhanced hierarchical taxonomy labeling tools.
2605.21029.paper_summary_zh: 利用大型語言模型（LLMs）進行自動化分類法構建，為潛在複雜領域的全面而高效的映射提供了明確的機會。<lb/>然而，當面對快速增長的高容量語料時，如何最佳利用這些數據以達成最佳的分類法構建變得不明朗。<lb/>以系統化工作場所中的人工智慧技能為例，我們使用兩個大規模的職位發布語料來研究在分類法構建中納入（或排除）數據點的關鍵設計決策。<lb/>我們提出TaxonomyBuilder作為我們系統研究的藍圖，通過它我們評估自定義、數據驅動和層級分類法的各種配置。<lb/>我們展示了較少的數據可以提供更多的清晰度：對TaxonomyBuilder的輸入進行過濾比提供未過濾的輸入給聚類和增強型層級分類法標記工具提供了更好的領域特定覆蓋。
2605.21029.author: Stephen Meisenbacher et.al.
